name: Empanaditas
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Cottage Cheese; Sm Curd, Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Hard boil an egg and peel it. Chop the hard boiled egg.
2. Cook ground beef in a 10-inch skillet until brown, breaking it up into small pieces. Drain, reserving 1 tablespoon of fat.
3. Stir in onion, raisins, olives, salt, and pepper. Cover and cook over low heat for 5 minutes.
4. Stir in cottage cheese and the chopped hard boiled egg.
5. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
6. Mix egg whites and water until slightly foamy; set aside.
7. Prepare pastry dough and shape it into two flattened rounds on a lightly floured surface.
8. Roll one round of pastry into a circle about 14 inches in diameter, then cut into 11 or 12 circles measuring 3.5 inches.
9. Spoon 2 tablespoons of beef mixture onto the center of each circle. Brush the edges with egg white mixture.
10. Fold the pastry over the filling and press the edge to seal. Place empanaditas on an ungreased cookie sheet.
11. Repeat steps 7-10 with remaining pastry rounds.
12. Roll up any leftover dough, shape into a round, roll out, cut, and fill as needed.
13. Beat egg yolk and milk until well blended; brush over the tops of empanaditas.
14. Bake in the preheated oven for 15 to 20 minutes or until golden brown. Serve warm.
